content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 17, 2016

Patricia Crane
Assistant Treasurer
1112 Montana Avenue, #358
Santa Monica, CA 90403

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-16-094

Dear Ms. Crane:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to
any person whose duties under the Act are in question. (Section 83114 (b); Regulation 18329(a).)
The Commission, however, may decline to provide advice where the advice would relate to past
conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) Because the deadline for submitting the committee
semiannual disclosure report was February 1, 2016, as advised in the formal advice letter issued to
you on January 20, 2016, any advice we provide would relate to past conduct. (Crane Advice
Letter, No. A-15-233.)

The January 20, 2016 advice letter stated:

The Act requires candidates and committees to file periodic reports disclosing
contributions received and expenditures made in connection with state and local
elections. "Committee" includes any person or group of persons that receives
contributions totaling $ 2,000 or more in a calendar year for the purpose of
supporting or opposing candidates or for the qualification, passage, or defeat of a
ballot measure.² (Sections 82013(a) and 82015; Regulation 18215.)

. . .

Under your facts, as of December 2, 2015, you have already formed a committee,
filed a Form 410 and received contributions from others for political purposes of
more than $1,000; so the committee is already reporting its contributions and

___________________________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² The Committee qualification threshold of Section 82013(a) increased from $1,000 to $2,000 on January 1,
2016 due to statutory changes.

File No. W-16-094
Page No. 2

expenditures. The next disclosure statement is due no later than February 1, 2016
covering the period through December 31, 2015.

(Crane Advice Letter, A-15-233)

You indicated that you also contacted the FPPC's advice line on January 29, 2016 about the
deadline for filing the committee's campaign statement. However, we provided formal written
advice specifically addressing your facts in a letter and this advice is accurate based on all the
information you provided about the committee's activities.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

By: [signature]
L. Karen Harrison
Counsel, Legal Division

KJH:jgl

sections.conclusion: The Commission declines to provide advice because the advice would relate to past conduct. The deadline for submitting the committee semiannual disclosure report was February 1, 2016, and formal written advice addressing the requestor's facts was already provided in Advice Letter No. A-15-233 dated January 20, 2016.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the FPPC would provide advice regarding committee reporting obligations and campaign disclosure deadlines under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice on this matter because any advice would address past conduct after the February 1, 2016 filing deadline, and the Commission had already issued formal written advice addressing the requestor's specific facts.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter (File No. W-16-094W indicates withdrawal/declination). No traditional QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; content structured as letter body. References prior formal advice letter A-15-233 issued January 20, 2016. Document provides regulatory citations and explains basis for declination.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the FPPC would provide advice regarding committee reporting obligations and campaign disclosure deadlines under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The Commission declines to provide advice because the advice would relate to past conduct. The deadline for submitting the committee semiannual disclosure report was February 1, 2016, and formal written advice addressing the requestor's facts was already provided in Advice Letter No. A-15-233 dated January 20, 2016.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide advice to Patricia Crane regarding campaign reporting obligations, citing Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) which allows the Commission to decline advice relating to past conduct. The Commission notes that formal advice was previously provided in letter A-15-233 dated January 20, 2016.
